Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a single household task that needs to be completed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task entity.

title.description: The concise name or title of the task.

description.description: A detailed description of the task, including any specific instructions.

category.description: The category the task belongs to (e.g., 'Cooking', 'Cleaning', 'Shopping', 'Maintenance').

dueDate.description: The date and time by which the task is expected to be completed.

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the task has been marked as completed.

completedDate.description: The date and time when the task was completed. Null if not completed.

isRepeating.description: Indicates if the task is a recurring task.

repeatFrequency.description: Specifies how often a recurring task repeats (e.g., 'DAILY', 'WEEKLY', 'MONTHLY', 'YEARLY', 'CUSTOM'). Required if isRepeating is true.

repeatEndsOn.description: The date when a repeating task should stop repeating. Null if it repeats indefinitely.

suggestedDishName.description: A dish name suggested by AI for cooking tasks, if applicable. (Optional)

createdAt.description: The date and time when the task was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the task was last updated.

Bill.title: Bill
Bill.description: Represents a household bill that needs to be tracked and paid.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bill entity.

amount.description: The total amount of the bill.

dueDate.description: The date by which the bill is due.

isPaid.description: Indicates whether the bill has been paid.

paidDate.description: The date when the bill was paid. Null if not paid.

paymentMethod.description: The method used or planned to be used for payment (e.g., 'Bank Transfer', 'Credit Card', 'Cash').

notes.description: Optional notes or remarks about the bill.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the bill record was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the bill record was last updated.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Represents a single expense incurred by the household.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

category.description: The category of the expense (e.g., 'Grocery', 'Transport', 'Dining Out', 'Utilities', 'Entertainment').

description.description: Optional notes or a brief description of the expense.

date.description: The date when the expense occurred.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the expense record was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the expense record was last updated.

Budget.title: Budget
Budget.description: Defines a financial budget for a specific period, allowing users to track overall spending.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Budget entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the budget (e.g., 'Monthly Household Budget', 'Vacation Savings').

startDate.description: The starting date of the budget period.

endDate.description: The ending date of the budget period.

totalAllocatedAmount.description: The total amount allocated for this budget period.

isCurrent.description: Indicates if this is the currently active budget.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the budget record was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the budget record was last updated.

BudgetCategoryLimit.title: BudgetCategoryLimit
BudgetCategoryLimit.description: Sets a spending limit for a specific category within a budget.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BudgetCategoryLimit entity.

budgetId.description: Reference to the Budget this limit belongs to. (Relationship: Budget 1:N BudgetCategoryLimit)

category.description: The expense category to which this limit applies (e.g., 'Grocery', 'Transport').

limitAmount.description: The maximum amount allocated for this category within the budget.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the budget category limit was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the budget category limit was last updated.

SavingsGoal.title: SavingsGoal
SavingsGoal.description: Represents a financial goal the user is saving towards.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SavingsGoal entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the savings goal (e.g., 'Emergency Fund', 'New Appliance').

targetAmount.description: The total amount the user aims to save.

currentAmount.description: The current amount saved towards the goal.

targetDate.description: The desired date by which the savings goal should be achieved. Null if no specific date.

notes.description: Optional notes or details about the savings goal.

isAchieved.description: Indicates whether the savings goal has been reached.

achievedDate.description: The date when the savings goal was achieved. Null if not yet achieved.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the savings goal was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the savings goal was last updated.

LocationReminder.title: LocationReminder
LocationReminder.description: Represents a reminder that triggers when the user enters or exits a specified geographical location.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LocationReminder entity.

title.description: A brief title for the location-based reminder (e.g., 'Grocery Shopping', 'Pick up Dry Cleaning').

message.description: The detailed message displayed when the reminder is triggered.

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the reminder location.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the reminder location.

radiusMeters.description: The radius in meters around the specified location where the reminder should trigger.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the location reminder is currently active.

associatedTaskId.description: Reference to a Task if this reminder is specifically tied to a task. (Relationship: Task 1:N LocationReminder)

createdAt.description: The date and time when the location reminder was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the location reminder was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual household tasks created and managed by a specific user. The 'userId' in the path serves as the owner identifier, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user (request.auth.uid) who owns this task.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique ID of the task.

definition.description: Stores household bills tracked by a specific user. The 'userId' in the path serves as the owner identifier, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user (request.auth.uid) who owns this bill.

params.name: billId
params.description: The unique ID of the bill.

definition.description: Stores individual expenses logged by a specific user. The 'userId' in the path serves as the owner identifier, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user (request.auth.uid) who logged this expense.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique ID of the expense.

definition.description: Stores financial budgets defined by a specific user. The 'userId' in the path serves as the owner identifier, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user (request.auth.uid) who owns this budget.

params.name: budgetId
params.description: The unique ID of the budget.

definition.description: Stores spending limits for categories within a user's specific budget. Each document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field matching the 'userId' from the path, ensuring authorization independence without requiring parent document reads.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user (request.auth.uid) who owns the parent budget and this limit.

params.name: budgetId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent budget to which this category limit belongs.

params.name: limitId
params.description: The unique ID of the budget category limit.

definition.description: Stores financial savings goals for a specific user. The 'userId' in the path serves as the owner identifier, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user (request.auth.uid) who owns this savings goal.

params.name: goalId
params.description: The unique ID of the savings goal.

definition.description: Stores location-based reminders for a specific user, which can optionally be associated with a task. The 'userId' in the path serves as the owner identifier, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user (request.auth.uid) who owns this reminder.

params.name: reminderId
params.description: The unique ID of the location reminder.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes user data isolation and strong authorization independence, adhering strictly to the CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES and DESIGN STRATEGY MANDATES. All user-specific data is nested under a `/users/{userId}` top-level collection, leveraging path-based ownership for inherently secure and debuggable rules.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by embedding the `userId` in the document path for all primary collections (e.g., `/users/{userId}/tasks/{taskId}`). For nested subcollections like `categoryLimits` under `budgets`, the `ownerId` (which is the `userId`) will be explicitly denormalized within each `BudgetCategoryLimit` document. This ensures that security rules for *any* document can directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` (or `request.auth.uid == userId` for top-level collections), completely eliminating the need for `get()` calls to parent documents. This design pattern allows for atomic write operations and simplifies rule logic considerably, making it robust against race conditions and easier to debug.

**QAPs (Query as Authorization Patterns):** The `/users/{userId}/...` hierarchical structure inherently supports secure `list` operations. A security rule such as `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;` will ensure that a user can only query and retrieve documents from their own designated path. The structural segregation of data by user makes it impossible for a query to accidentally expose another user's data, as the `userId` wildcard acts as a mandatory filter that matches the authenticated user's ID. Each collection (`tasks`, `bills`, `expenses`, `budgets`, `savingsGoals`, `locationReminders`) operates with a homogeneous security posture, owned exclusively by the `userId` in the path, thus simplifying rules for `list` as well as `get`, `create`, `update`, and `delete` operations.
